mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 (including FLT3-ITD) and PKC signaling, reducing AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Approved in FLT3+ AML; mentioned as inhibiting FLT3-ITD AML lines including MOLM13 in many reports

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL kinases to suppress survival/proliferation signaling in FLT3-driven AML
additional_notes: Approved in relapsed/refractory FLT3 AML; potency described as single-digit to tens of nM (upper bound not explicitly specified)

mechanism: Selective FLT3 inhibition, particularly effective in FLT3-ITD AML models
additional_notes: Approved in some regions/clinical; potency frequently ~1–10 nM in FLT3-ITD AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases (includes RAF pathway and can inhibit FLT3), impairing AML growth in some contexts
additional_notes: Used off-label in FLT3-ITD AML; potency described as variable

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family kinases and ABL, affecting signaling pathways that can support AML cell survival
additional_notes: AML activity in subsets; sometimes reported synergistic with FLT3 inhibition

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the RAS/MAPK pathway, reducing proliferation in MAPK-driven AML subsets
additional_notes: Approved; activity noted in RAS/MAPK-driven AML subsets

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 signaling, often producing cytostatic effects in AML
additional_notes: Approved; combinations often stronger than single-agent per text

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling (mTORC1), impacting cell growth and metabolism
additional_notes: Approved; potency described as similar to everolimus class range

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, promoting transcriptional changes leading to growth arrest/apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also referred to as SAHA (alternative name in parentheses)

mechanism: Pan-HDAC inhibition leading to altered chromatin/transcription and apoptosis
additional_notes: Noted as more potent than vorinostat/SAHA

mechanism: Inhibits HDACs, altering gene expression and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved; potency described as ~1–50 nM

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferases, causing hypomethylation and altered gene expression
additional_notes: Approved in myeloid malignancies; potency/time-response strongly exposure-time dependent per text

mechanism: Incorporates into RNA and DNA and reduces DNA methylation via DNMT inhibition, affecting transcription and viability
additional_notes: Approved; effect depends on exposure schedule/time per text

mechanism: Selective BCL-2 inhibition triggers mitochondrial apoptosis in susceptible AML cells
additional_notes: Approved; described as AML backbone in combinations; resistance can relate to MCL-1 dependence

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2 family proteins (BCL-2 and BCL-XL) to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Also referred to as ABT-263 (alternative name in parentheses); platelet toxicity noted clinically

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved; variable single-agent AML activity; combinations often better per text

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits proteasome activity, inducing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved; potency described as often ~5–50 nM

mechanism: Inhibits exportin-1 (XPO1/CRM1), retaining tumor suppressors in the nucleus and promoting apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved; described as pro-apoptotic in AML models and in trials for AML

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increasing oxidative stress (ROS) and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Repurposed non-oncology candidate; AML evidence across multiple AML line studies

mechanism: Reported ALDH-related and copper/metal-dependent cytotoxic mechanisms leading to stress/apoptosis in AML under certain conditions
additional_notes: Activity reported especially with copper; described as highly condition-dependent
